nodes.label: Anaplan vs Varicent (SPM Platform Comparison)
nodes.summary: Two leading vendors in the sales performance management (SPM) market, evaluated head-to-head using the Consulting-A [[Vendor-Selection-Playbook]] framework (7-category weighted scoring, evidence-first governance). The comparison surfaces a

nodes.label: Hunter Plan vs Multi-Stream Plan
nodes.summary: Two fundamentally different plan architectures differing in **how many independent revenue/contribution streams a rep is incentivized on**:

nodes.label: Material Risk — Narrow (Proxy-Disclosure) vs Broad (Component-Math) Reading
nodes.summary: Two mechanically-distinct readings of the phrase "material risk" as it appears across different governance artifacts for the same company and the same comp program. The DentalDist-A governance package surfaces this tension directly: a proxy

nodes.label: Tiered Accelerator vs Linear (Uncapped) Acceleration
nodes.summary: Two fundamentally different shapes for the variable portion of a sales compensation plan, with distinct diagnostic profiles and typical industry fits.

nodes.label: 14-Dimension Design Framework (Comp Plan Clarification)
nodes.summary: A structured methodology for converting a rep-facing (or informally-documented) compensation plan into an **engineering-grade clarified specification**. The framework walks through 14 explicit dimensions, each of which must be resolved with

nodes.label: AR-Aging Deduction
nodes.summary: A progressive penalty mechanic that deducts a percentage from a seller's monthly commissions based on the aging of customer accounts receivable (AR) in their territory. As AR ages past defined day-buckets (e.g., 60, 90, 120, 150 days past d

nodes.label: Access Controls Model (RBAC for Compensation Systems)
nodes.summary: A **role-based access control (RBAC)** model for compensation systems, with enumerated roles, tiered approval provisioning, quarterly manager certification, time-boxed temporary access, and break-glass emergency access with full audit trail

nodes.label: Administrative Eligibility Gate
nodes.summary: A payout condition that depends not on sales attainment but on **operational compliance** — completing an administrative action (device registration, contract filing, data entry) by a deadline.

nodes.label: All-Quarter Kicker (Back-to-Dollar-One Retroactive Acceleration)
nodes.summary: A retroactive payout mechanic where crossing an attainment threshold in a given period **increases the commission rate applied to the entire period's revenue from the first dollar** — not just the portion above the threshold. The "all-quart

nodes.label: Annual Restoration
nodes.summary: A payout mechanic where under-performing periods (typically quarters) are **retroactively restored to full payout** at year-end if an annual threshold (typically 100% of annual goal) is met. Unlike a simple annual true-up that pays a delta

nodes.label: California Labor Code Compliance
nodes.summary: A compliance concept covering the **California-specific statutory requirements** for commission-based compensation that typically exceed federal protections. CA law provides greater employee protections in multiple areas — final pay timing,

nodes.label: Cliff vs Smooth Thresholds
nodes.summary: The design choice between a **cliff threshold** (payout jumps instantly from $0 to some positive value at exactly the threshold attainment) and a **smooth threshold** (payout ramps up gradually from $0 as attainment approaches and crosses t

nodes.label: Commission Floor Guarantee
nodes.summary: A plan mechanic that establishes a minimum commission payout per transaction, regardless of the commission rate that would otherwise apply. The seller receives the **higher of** the standard commission calculation or the floor guarantee. Th

nodes.label: Commission-Rate Plan
nodes.summary: A compensation plan where the seller earns a percentage of a revenue or profit measure (typically Gross Profit Dollars) rather than a bonus calculated from quota attainment. The fundamental difference from a quota-attainment bonus plan: the

nodes.label: Compensation Review Board (CRB)
nodes.summary: The **operational** governance body for sales compensation plan design, approval, and oversight. Approves plan designs, plan changes, individual exceptions, and escalated disputes within dollar/percentage thresholds. Reports up to the [[Gov

nodes.label: Compensation Risk Assessment (Proxy Disclosure + Operational Inventory)
nodes.summary: A **dual-purpose governance process** combining:

nodes.label: Competitive-Displacement Commission
nodes.summary: A commission-rate variant that pays a **reduced rate on competitive-displacement sales up to a prior-year-spend threshold, then a full (or enhanced) rate on incremental sales above that threshold**. The goal is to incentivize winning accoun

nodes.label: Consulting Practice Lead Structure
nodes.summary: A plan shape specific to consulting and professional-services firms where a senior individual (often titled Practice Lead, Practice Director, Vertical Head, or similar) is compensated for building and running a revenue-generating practice.

nodes.label: Cross-Category Gate
nodes.summary: A payout gate requiring minimum achievement in a "sister" product category to unlock bonus eligibility in the primary category. Typically structured as: "To earn the Core overachievement bonus, you must achieve at least X% in Digital (and v

nodes.label: Cross-Measure Conflict
nodes.summary: A plan-design failure mode in [[Multi-Measure-Weighted-Split]] architectures where **two or more measures have incentives that pull in opposite directions** — optimizing for one measure mechanically degrades attainment on another. The rep i

nodes.label: Cross-Product Commission Kicker
nodes.summary: A payout modifier where **hitting attainment thresholds on multiple measures simultaneously** unlocks incremental commission rate bumps on each measure. Unlike a simple per-measure accelerator, the kicker is **conditional on breadth** — you

nodes.label: Deal-Team Link Archetype
nodes.summary: A canonical-schema archetype for plans where **multiple reps contribute to a single deal** and must share credit and commission. The archetype captures the data-model relationships required to express deal-team-based credit splitting in a s

nodes.label: Decelerator Below Threshold
nodes.summary: A mechanic where commission rate drops BELOW the base rate when attainment falls below a specific threshold. Functions as a "punishment zone" — reps who miss target earn something, but at a reduced rate that signals the company's dissatisfa

nodes.label: Employment-at-Period-End Forfeiture
nodes.summary: A plan rule specifying that a rep who is not employed at the end of a pay period forfeits all commission earned during that period. The rule treats active employment at the specific calendar date of period close as a **condition of payment*

nodes.label: Equipment Clawback
nodes.summary: A recovery mechanic where commission previously earned on equipment sales is **reversed or deducted from future payouts** when certain post-sale events occur: product returns, aged receivables that are written off, or bad-debt reversals. Sp

nodes.label: Event-Driven Release Payout (Calc-and-Release)
nodes.summary: A mechanic where commission is **calculated at one event** (the booking) but **released to the rep across multiple subsequent events** (shipment, payment, etc.) according to fixed percentages. The total commission accrues at booking for acc

nodes.label: Flat-Rate Commission
nodes.summary: A compensation measure where participants earn a **fixed percentage of revenue** with no threshold floor, no tiers, and no attainment-dependent rate changes. Earnings begin at the first dollar of revenue.

nodes.label: Floor-To-Threshold Migration
nodes.summary: A deliberate plan-design evolution where a company **replaces a "floor" (guaranteed minimum payout)** with a **"threshold" (zero-payout below a stated attainment percentage)**. The floor and threshold occupy the same structural position in

nodes.label: Goal-Factor Multiplier
nodes.summary: A quota-setting mechanic where the seller's annual goal is computed as a fixed multiple of their base salary: `Goal = Base Salary × Goal Factor`. The goal factor varies by plan level, typically with an **inverse relationship**: lower base s

nodes.label: Governance Committee (Strategic Tier)
nodes.summary: The **strategic** governance body for sales compensation programs. Sets policy, approves frameworks, oversees risk, approves major investments (technology, consulting), and reports up to the Board and Audit Committee. Sits **above** the [[C

nodes.label: Growth-Over-Baseline Model
nodes.summary: A compensation plan where no variable pay is earned on base territory sales. Variable pay is triggered only by incremental sales above a fixed dollar baseline ("growth quota"). The seller's entire variable opportunity is keyed to growth vs.

nodes.label: Hard Cap Ceiling
nodes.summary: A plan mechanic that bounds maximum payout per rep per period, expressed either as an absolute dollar amount (`maxPayout`) or as a multiple of variable OTE (`maxMultipleOfOTE`). Once a rep's calculated commission would exceed the ceiling, i

nodes.label: Inside Sales Plan Structure
nodes.summary: A compensation plan architecture for **non-field (inside) sales roles** that differs structurally from field sales plans through lower targets, lower thresholds, quality gates, and team-level performance bonuses.

nodes.label: Inverse Expense Bonus
nodes.summary: A compensation component where **lower spending earns higher payouts**. Structurally the opposite of a revenue accelerator — the best performers are the ones who spend the least relative to budget.

nodes.label: KPMG Incentive Compensation Framework
nodes.summary: KPMG's **5-area framework** for effective sales incentive compensation design. A strategic / governance framing (vs. Consulting-A's more tactical design + analytics approach). Positions sales comp as an **enabler of sales strategy** — not a

nodes.label: Longevity Multiplier
nodes.summary: A compensation mechanic that scales a bonus component based on the seller's tenure (years of service). Longer tenure earns a higher multiplier on a performance-based bonus, creating a retention incentive that compounds with performance. The

nodes.label: Management Override Structures (Direct + Indirect)
nodes.summary: A plan mechanic where managers earn commission based on the performance of their subordinates. Overrides come in two structural flavors that are often conflated but must be distinguished:

nodes.label: Margin Gate
nodes.summary: A payout gate that **withholds commission earnings on deals or periods failing a margin-profitability threshold**. Unlike a quota-attainment threshold (which gates on revenue performance), a margin gate tests whether the revenue was generat

nodes.label: Milestone Kicker
nodes.summary: A **flat-dollar bonus** paid when a rep crosses a specific attainment milestone (typically 100% of quota, but also seen at 80%, 125%, etc.). The bonus amount is fixed — unrelated to the exact attainment level above the threshold — and it cr

nodes.label: Mixed Interpretation Mode (Marginal + Effective)
nodes.summary: A plan that uses BOTH marginal interpretation on base rates AND effective interpretation on acceleration multipliers. The two interpretations solve different problems at different parts of the payout curve:

nodes.label: Monthly YTD Payout Model
nodes.summary: A payout settlement mechanic where incentives are calculated and paid **monthly** based on year-to-date actuals, with each month's payment equal to YTD earnings minus all previous payments. No draw is used.

nodes.label: Multi-Component Uncapped Bonus
nodes.summary: A plan where variable compensation is divided across multiple INDEPENDENT uncapped bonus pools, each tied to a different metric. Distinct from [[Multi-Measure-Weighted-Split]] because each component is a separately calculated and separately

nodes.label: Multi-Measure Split 80/20 (Behavioral — New vs Renewal)
nodes.summary: A specific instance of [[Multi-Measure-Weighted-Split]] with an **80/20 split between New Net ARR (NNA)** and **Renewal ARR (RNW)** — the canonical SaaS AE comp structure that allocates 80% of variable compensation to new-logo / new-arr acq

nodes.label: Multi-Measure Weighted Split
nodes.summary: A plan that pays against two or more measures (e.g. New Logo ARR + Renewal ARR + Services) with explicit weight fractions that must sum to 1.0. Each measure has its own target (quota for that measure) and its own credit rules. The variable

nodes.label: Multi-Position Payee Model (Tri-Part: Payee / Position / Role)
nodes.summary: A data model where the three concepts most comp systems collapse into one — "the rep" — are explicitly separated into three independent entities:

nodes.label: Multi-Product Portfolio Weighting
nodes.summary: A variant of [[Multi-Measure-Weighted-Split]] where the weighted measures correspond to **product families or SKU categories** rather than to behavioral metrics (new vs renewal) or organizational scope (individual vs team vs division). The

nodes.label: National-Level Quota Alignment
nodes.summary: A plan design in which the measure attainment is computed against a **national** quota (aggregated across all geographies in the country) rather than against the specific seller's territory, district, area, or region. The seller's payout mo

nodes.label: Non-Recoverable Draw
nodes.summary: A draw advance where the employer does **not** recover the overpayment if actual earned incentives fall short of the draw amount. The employee keeps the draw regardless of performance.

nodes.label: Operating Margin Measure
nodes.summary: A plan measure scored against **operating margin percentage** (profitability) rather than revenue, units, or expense absolute dollars. Typically implemented as a **lookup table** mapping attainment percentage to payout percentage, with a fl

nodes.label: Overachievement Withheld Until Year-End
nodes.summary: A payout-cadence mechanic where variable compensation for the **0%–100% attainment band** is paid on the standard cadence (usually quarterly), but variable compensation for **overachievement (101%+)** is calculated and paid only **after the

nodes.label: Per-Transaction Commission
nodes.summary: A compensation mechanic where the commission rate is determined independently for each individual transaction (delivery, order, deal) based on that transaction's characteristics, rather than being determined by aggregate period-level attain

nodes.label: Per-Unit Incremental Bonus
nodes.summary: A compensation component that pays a **fixed dollar amount per unit** (e.g., per implant, per installation) after exceeding a baseline count, rather than using percentage-of-revenue or quota attainment.

nodes.label: Plan Design Methodology
nodes.summary: The **process of designing a compensation plan from strategy down to mechanics**, as taught by Consulting-A. The methodology organizes design decisions into three pillars — **Measures & Weights**, **Mechanics**, **Crediting** — and overlays

nodes.label: Plan Spec vs Clarified Design
nodes.summary: The **distinction between the raw compensation plan document as it's delivered to reps** (plan spec — often narrative, ambiguous, with implicit assumptions) and **the fully-unambiguous operational design that a comp administrator, calculati

nodes.label: Product Segmentation Strategy
nodes.summary: A plan design pattern where **individual products or product families receive their own dedicated compensation components** rather than being measured as a single revenue pool. Used to steer sales focus toward strategic priorities.

nodes.label: Q4-Only Uncapped
nodes.summary: A plan design where caps ([[Hard-Cap-Ceiling]] or [[Soft-Cap-Review-Gate]]) apply to **Q1–Q3 individually** but **Q4 is uncapped** — allowing any amount of over-performance in the final quarter to flow through without truncation or review g

nodes.label: Quartile Payout Ranges
nodes.summary: A payout-determination mechanism where rep comp is assigned based on **which quartile (or tier band) they land in** within a performance ranking, with payout falling **within a dollar range** specific to that band. Position within the range

nodes.label: Quota Management Methodology
nodes.summary: The **process of forecasting, allocating, and managing individual and territory quotas** such that they reflect true addressable opportunity and support performance management. Consulting-A (BHG) frames quota setting as a joint problem of f

nodes.label: Recoverable Draw
nodes.summary: A periodic **advance against future commissions** — the rep receives a fixed guaranteed payment each pay period (weekly, monthly, or quarterly), and earned commissions are first used to repay the accumulated draw before any above-draw earni

nodes.label: Recovery Precedence (Overpayment-First, Then Draw)
nodes.summary: A plan rule specifying the ORDER in which negative compensation deltas are applied to a rep's earnings, and WHICH streams each recovery type can draw from. Two mechanisms typically need recovery:

nodes.label: Reference Call Methodology
nodes.summary: Standardized protocol for interviewing vendor-supplied customer references during the Due Diligence phase of the [[Vendor-Selection-Playbook]]. Ten core questions organized around six major risk dimensions, with a structured note-capture fo

nodes.label: Regressive Acceleration
nodes.summary: A plan mechanic where the payout multiplier increases through mid-range attainment tiers but then **decreases** at the highest tiers. The multiplier pattern peaks mid-range (e.g., 3x at 116--130%) and drops back (e.g., 2x at 130%+), creatin

nodes.label: Risk-Scoring Framework
nodes.summary: A **quantitative risk-scoring methodology** for compensation-plan components. Each component is scored 1–5 across five independent risk dimensions; the dimensions sum to a total score out of 25. Total scores categorize components into CRITI

nodes.label: SPM Analytics Framework
nodes.summary: The **diagnostic analytics toolkit** used by Consulting-A (BHG) for assessing an existing sales compensation program and building an evidence base for plan redesign recommendations. Not a design framework — an **analytical lens** pointed at

nodes.label: Sales Comp Terms and Conditions Framework
nodes.summary: The **governance layer** of a sales compensation program — the standard clauses that appear in a plan document *outside* the mechanics (measures, weights, rates, tiers). Covers commission timing, recovery rules, split credit, windfall busin

nodes.label: Sandbag Risk
nodes.summary: The behavioral tendency for sales reps to **deliberately delay or withhold deal closure** when doing so is financially advantageous under their comp plan. Most commonly triggered by hard caps (rep at cap in current period pushes deals to ne

nodes.label: Security Due Diligence Framework
nodes.summary: A **seven-section structured checklist** for performing security due diligence on shortlisted SPM/ICM vendors during Phase 5 of the [[Vendor-Selection-Playbook]]. The framework is deliberately **prompt-based rather than prescriptive** — eac

nodes.label: Segregation of Duties (SoD)
nodes.summary: The governance principle that **no single role should perform all steps** of a sensitive process (plan design, calculation, approval, payment). Enforced via a **RACI matrix** (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed) across the full c

nodes.label: Short-Dated Product Bonus
nodes.summary: A per-unit bonus paid for selling, registering, and implanting products within a short window (typically 0-120 days) before their Use By Date. An inventory-management incentive that rewards clinical/field staff for moving near-expiry stock

nodes.label: Single-Measure Plans
nodes.summary: A plan where 100% of variable compensation is tied to a single measure. No split across measures, no weighted pool, no independent components. The rep's entire variable comp rises and falls with one number.

nodes.label: Soft Cap with Executive Approval (Attainment Variant)
nodes.summary: A soft-cap mechanism where calculated payout above a **specified attainment percentage** (typically 100%–125% of quota) requires **named executive approval** to be paid. The rep's math stands — they earned the amount — but the payout is hel

nodes.label: Soft Cap Review Gate
nodes.summary: A mechanic where a rep's calculated payout above a **dollar threshold** is FLAGGED FOR HUMAN REVIEW rather than automatically truncated. The rep's calculation produces the full earned amount, but the payout is placed in a **held status** pe

nodes.label: Stack-Rank Performance Plan
nodes.summary: A fundamentally different plan architecture in which **payout is determined by peer ranking rather than individual quota attainment**. Reps are stack-ranked within a peer cohort (ORG, region, team), and comp is assigned based on rank positi

nodes.label: Stacking Premiums
nodes.summary: A commission architecture where multiple **additive premium layers** stack on top of a base commission rate, each layer triggered by specific deal characteristics (strategic customer, preferred vendor, premium brand, etc.). Each premium is

nodes.label: Stakeholder Communication Framework
nodes.summary: A governance framework for all sales-compensation-related communications: plan rollouts, ongoing operations, dispute response, executive reporting, crisis protocol. Combines **annual calendar, standardized templates, tiered SLAs, feedback m

nodes.label: Taxonomy Dictionary (Controlled Vocabulary)
nodes.summary: A **standardized terminology dictionary** for compensation concepts, maintained as a governance artifact. All plan documentation and communications must reference these standard definitions. New terms are added through a governance process,

nodes.label: Territory Management Methodology
nodes.summary: The **process of defining, sizing, valuing, and assigning sales territories** as an integrated input to quota setting and comp plan design. Consulting-A's (BHG) TQM framework treats territory as the foundation: before you can set a quota or

nodes.label: Three-Tier Revenue Architecture (Individual / Team / Division)
nodes.summary: A compensation structure where variable comp is split across three **concentric organizational revenue layers**: the rep's own revenue, the rep's team/category revenue, and the division-wide (or multi-team) revenue. Each layer has an indepe

nodes.label: Threshold Cliff
nodes.summary: A plan mechanic where crossing a specific attainment threshold triggers a discontinuous jump in payout. The most common form is a **floor threshold**: below `minimumAttainment`, variable comp is ZERO. At or above the threshold, variable com

nodes.label: Tiered Accelerator Plans
nodes.summary: A compensation plan shape where the base commission rate is multiplied by progressively larger factors as attainment crosses defined thresholds. Each tier specifies an `thresholdAttainment` (e.g. 1.0 = 100% of quota) and a `multiplier` (e.g

nodes.label: Uncapped Linear Acceleration
nodes.summary: A plan shape where commission earnings scale LINEARLY with credited revenue (or gross profit, or another metric) with NO mechanical upper bound. No tier boundaries, no multipliers, no cap. A rep who earns 2x target revenue earns exactly 2x

nodes.label: Vacant Position Coverage
nodes.summary: A compensation component that pays a **weekly bonus for covering a vacant territory** left by a departed colleague, incentivizing voluntary coverage during hiring gaps.

nodes.label: Vendor Demo Script Framework
nodes.summary: Methodology for running **scenario-based vendor demos** in Phase 4 of the [[Vendor-Selection-Playbook]]. Replaces vendor-controlled narrative demos with client-scripted scenarios that prove specific requirements under standardized condition

nodes.label: Vendor Selection Playbook
nodes.summary: End-to-end methodology for running an SPM / ICM vendor selection engagement. Takes a client from kickoff through a signed-off vendor recommendation backed by evidence, gates, weighted scoring, ROM, TCO, and a decision log. Authored by [[Ent

nodes.label: YTD (Year-to-Date) Rollup Payout
nodes.summary: A payout cycle where commission is calculated against **cumulative year-to-date attainment**, not period-by-period attainment. Each payout period (typically quarterly) looks at the rep's total revenue against their total quota through that

nodes.label: Year-End True-Up Guarantee
nodes.summary: A mechanic where if a participant meets the **annual** performance threshold but their **quarterly** payouts sum to less than the annual target, the difference is paid at fiscal year-end.

nodes.label: Medical Devices

nodes.label: Inside Sales Consultant (ISC) - Medical Distribution
